title: Sacoglottis oblongifolia
intro: Sacoglottis oblongifolia, also known as the Amazonian tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Humiriaceae. The health benefits of Sacoglottis oblongifolia include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Sacoglottis oblongifolia is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction to treat various health conditions, and its leaves are also used in traditional medicine to make capsules and tinctures.

This family is part of the larger clade that includes many tropical and subtropical plant species. Within the family Calophyllaceae, Sacoglottis oblongifolia is a member of the genus Sacoglottis, which consists of several species of trees and shrubs. The classification of Sacoglottis oblongifolia is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and fruit structure.
